title: भगवद्गीता अध्याय १२ - भक्तियोग
devanagariText:
॥ अथ द्वादशोऽध्यायः - भक्तियोगः ॥

अर्जुन उवाच —
एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते।
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः॥१॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।
श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः॥२॥

ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते।
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम्॥३॥

सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः।
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः॥४॥

क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम्।
अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते॥५॥

ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि सन्न्यस्य मत्पराः।
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते॥६॥

तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्।
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्॥७॥

मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय।
निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः॥८॥

अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्।
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय॥९॥

अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव।
मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि॥१०॥

अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान्॥११॥

श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते।
ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम्॥१२॥

अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी॥१३॥

सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः॥१४॥

यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः॥१५॥

अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः॥१६॥

यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः॥१७॥

समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः॥१८॥

तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येनकेनचित्।
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः॥१९॥

ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते।
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः॥२०॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतासूपनिषत्सु ब्रह्मविद्यायां योगशास्त्रे
श्रीकृष्णार्जुनसंवादे भक्तियोगो नाम द्वादशोऽध्यायः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Dvadasho'dhyayah - Bhakti Yogah ||

Arjuna Uvacha —
Evam Satata-Yukta Ye Bhaktas-Tvam Paryupasate.
Ye Cha-Apy-Aksharam-Avyaktam Tesham Ke Yoga-Vittamah.||1||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Mayy-Aveshya Mano Ye Mam Nitya-Yukta Upasate.
Shraddhaya Parayo-Upetas-Te Me Yuktatama Matah.||2||

Ye Tv-Aksharam-Anirdeshyam-Avyaktam Paryupasate.
Sarvatra-Gam-Achintyam Cha Kutastham-Achalam Dhruvam.||3||

Sanniyamye-Indriya-Gramam Sarvatra Sama-Buddhayah.
Te Prapnuvanti Mam-Eva Sarva-Bhuta-Hite Ratah.||4||

Klesho'dhikataras-Tesham-Avyakta-Asakta-Chetasam.
Avyakta Hi Gatir-Duhkham Deha-Vadbhir-Avapyate.||5||

Ye Tu Sarvani Karmani Mayi Sannyasya Mat-Parah.
Ananyena-Eva Yogena Mam Dhyayanta Upasate.||6||

Tesham-Aham Samuddharta Mrityu-Samsara-Sagarat.
Bhavami Na-Chirat Partha Mayy-Aveshita-Chetasam.||7||

Mayy-Eva Mana Adhatsva Mayi Buddhim Niveshaya.
Nivasisyasi Mayy-Eva Ata Urdhvam Na Samshayah.||8||

Atha Chittam Samadhatum Na Shaknoshi Mayi Sthiram.
Abhyasa-Yogena Tato Mam-Ichchha-Aptum Dhananjaya.||9||

Abhyase'py-Asamartho'si Mat-Karma-Paramo Bhava.
Mad-Artham-Api Karmani Kurvan-Siddhim-Avapsyasi.||10||

Atha-Itad-Apy-Ashakto'si Kartum Mad-Yogam-Ashritah.
Sarva-Karma-Phala-Tyagam Tatah Kuru Yata-Atmavan.||11||

Shreyo Hi Jnanam-Abhyasaj-Jnanad-Dhyanam Vishishyate.
Dhyanat-Karma-Phala-Tyagas-Tyagach-Chhantir-Anantaram.||12||

Adveshta Sarva-Bhutanam Maitrah Karuna Eva Cha.
Nirmamo Nir-Ahankaarah Sama-Duhkha-Sukhah Kshami.||13||

Santushtah Satatam Yogi Yata-Atma Dridha-Nishchayah.
Mayy-Arpita-Mano-Buddhir-Yo Mad-Bhaktah Sa Me Priyah.||14||

Yasman-No-Udvijate Loko Lokan-No-Udvijate Cha Yah.
Harsha-Amarsha-Bhayo-Dvegair-Mukto Yah Sa Cha Me Priyah.||15||

Anapekshah Shuchir-Daksha Udasino Gata-Vyathah.
Sarva-Arambha-Parityagi Yo Mad-Bhaktah Sa Me Priyah.||16||

Yo Na Hrishyati Na Dveshti Na Shochati Na Kankshati.
Shubha-Ashubha-Parityagi Bhaktiman-Yah Sa Me Priyah.||17||

Samah Shatrau Cha Mitre Cha Tatha Mana-Apamanayoh.
Shito-Ushna-Sukha-Duhkheshu Samah Sanga-Vivarjitah.||18||

Tulya-Ninda-Stutir-Mauni Santushto Yena-Kenachit.
Aniketah Sthira-Matir-Bhaktiman-Me Priyo Narah.||19||

Ye Tu Dharmya-Amritam-Idam Yatho-Uktam Paryupasate.
Shraddadhana Mat-Parama Bhaktas-Te'tiva Me Priyah.||20||

|| Iti Shrimad-Bhagavad-Gitasu-Upanishatsu Brahma-Vidyayam Yoga-Shastre
Shri-Krishna-Arjuna-Samvade Bhakti-Yogo Nama Dvadasho'dhyayah ||
hindiMeaning: द्वादश अध्याय 'भक्तियोग' गीता का सबसे अधिक पठित और हृदयस्पर्शी अध्याय है। अर्जुन पूछते हैं — सगुण-साकार भक्त और निर्गुण-निराकार उपासक में कौन श्रेष्ठ है? कृष्ण कहते हैं — जो मुझमें मन लगाकर श्रद्धा से उपासना करते हैं, वे मुझे सबसे श्रेष्ठ योगी मान्य हैं। मैं उनका मृत्यु-संसार-सागर से शीघ्र उद्धार करता हूँ। फिर कृष्ण भक्ति की सीढ़ियाँ बताते हैं — मन मुझमें लगाओ, यदि न कर सको तो अभ्यासयोग करो, यदि वह भी न हो तो मेरे लिए कर्म करो, यदि वह भी न हो तो कर्मफल का त्याग करो — त्याग से शान्ति मिलती है। अंत में भगवान को प्रिय भक्त के लक्षण बताते हैं — सब प्राणियों से द्वेषरहित, मैत्रीपूर्ण, करुणावान, ममतारहित, निरहंकारी, सुख-दुःख में समान, क्षमाशील, सदा संतुष्ट, दृढ़ निश्चयी, निन्दा-स्तुति में समान, मौनी — ऐसा भक्त मुझे अत्यंत प्रिय है।
benefits: The most recited chapter — supreme guide to devotion (Bhakti)
Reveals the qualities most dear to God in a devotee
Provides a step-by-step path suitable for all levels of seekers
Teaches that God personally rescues His devotees from suffering
Develops qualities of compassion, forgiveness, and equanimity
Traditionally associated with immediate peace through surrender and devotion
Short and powerful — ideal for daily recitation
description: Dedicated to Lord Krishna, the Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 - Bhakti Yoga (भगवद्गीता अध्याय १२ - भक्तियोग) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata. Regular recitation is believed to bring immediate peace through surrender and devotion, develop qualities of compassion, forgiveness, and equanimity, and short and powerful — ideal for daily recitation. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami for maximum spiritual benefit. At 20 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 - Bhakti Yoga in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 — Bhakti Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 (भगवद्गीता अध्याय १२), titled Bhakti Yoga (the Yoga of Devotion), is the twelfth chapter of the Srimad Bhagavad Gita and the shortest chapter of the entire text — only 20 verses. It is therefore the most popular for daily parayana. After the awesome Vishvarupa Darshana of Chapter 11, Arjuna asks: between the devotee who worships you in the saguna (with-form) personal form and the yogi who meditates on the akshara avyakta nirguna (formless unmanifest) Brahman, which is the better knower of yoga? Krishna answers that those who worship him with personal devotion, fixing their mind on him, are most perfectly united in yoga (verse 12.2). He then describes the path of devotion in stages — direct meditation, abhyasa-yoga (repeated practice), karma-yoga (offering all action to him), and finally renunciation of the fruits of action — and concludes with the celebrated qualities of the devotee dear to him (12.13-12.20). (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12; Mahabharata Bhishma Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Chapter 12 the most popular chapter for daily recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 12 is the most popular daily-recitation chapter of the Bhagavad Gita for several reasons: (1) it is the shortest at only 20 verses (about 10 minutes), making it accessible for daily morning or evening parayana; (2) it directly teaches Bhakti Yoga — the path most accessible to grihasthas and modern practitioners; (3) it culminates in the qualities of a true devotee (12.13-12.20), which serve as a daily ethical and spiritual checklist; (4) Adi Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva and Sant Gyaneshwar all single it out as the chapter for daily life; (5) Mahatma Gandhi recited Chapter 12 every evening as his daily spiritual practice and considered the qualities of the devotee verses (Adveshta sarva-bhutanam... onwards) as the supreme code of conduct. Many Hindu families recite Chapter 12 every evening before dinner alongside Gita verses 15. (Source: classical Bhagavad Gita parayana tradition; Gandhi's daily prayer routine.)

seoFaqs.question: Which is easier — saguna or nirguna worship — according to Chapter 12?
seoFaqs.answer: In verses 12.3-12.5 Krishna answers Arjuna's question on saguna versus nirguna worship: those who worship the akshara (imperishable), the anirdeshyam (indefinable), the avyaktam (unmanifest), the sarvatraga (all-pervasive), the achintyam (inconceivable), the kutastham (immutable), the achalam (unmoving), the dhruvam (eternal) — restraining all the senses, even-minded everywhere, intent on the welfare of all beings — also reach Krishna. But, he adds, the difficulty of those whose minds are fixed on the unmanifest is greater (klesho'dhikataras-tesham avyaktasakta-chetasam) — for the embodied being (dehavadbhih) attains the goal of the unmanifest with hardship. Saguna worship — directing love towards a personal divine form — is therefore the easier and more natural path for embodied human beings, even though both reach the same supreme goal. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 12.2-12.5.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the four-step ladder of devotion taught in Chapter 12?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 12.8-12.12 give a graded sadhana for the seeker who cannot immediately fix the mind on Krishna: (1) Step One (12.8) — fix the mind directly on me, immerse the buddhi in me; you will dwell in me thereafter, no doubt. (2) Step Two — if you cannot fix the mind firmly, then by abhyasa-yoga (the yoga of repeated practice), seek to attain me. (3) Step Three (12.10) — if you cannot do abhyasa, perform actions for my sake; even by doing works for me alone you will attain perfection. (4) Step Four (12.11) — if even this is not possible, take refuge in my yoga and renounce the fruits of all your actions, with self-control. (5) Verse 12.12 sums up: knowledge is better than mere practice, meditation is better than knowledge, and renunciation of the fruit of action (karma-phala-tyaga) is better than meditation — for from such renunciation peace immediately follows (tyagat shanti-r-anantaram). This four-step ladder makes Bhakti Yoga accessible to every level of capacity. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 12.8-12.12.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the qualities of the devotee dear to Krishna in Chapter 12?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 12.13-12.20 list the celebrated qualities of the devotee who is exceedingly dear (priyo'si me) to Krishna: (1) adveshta sarva-bhutanam — non-hating of all beings; maitra (friendly) and karuna (compassionate) without possessiveness or ego; (2) santushta satatam yogi — ever-content, self-controlled, of firm conviction, with mind and intellect dedicated to me; (3) yasman-nodvijate loko lokan-nodvijate cha yah — one who neither agitates the world nor is agitated by the world, free from joy, anger, fear and anxiety; (4) anapekshah shuchih daksho udasinah — unconcerned, pure, skilful, indifferent (to praise/blame), free from all undertakings; (5) yo na hrishyati na dveshti — one who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor desires, who has renounced both good and evil; (6) samah shatrau cha mitre cha — same to friend and foe, in honour and dishonour, in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, free from attachment; (7) tulya-ninda-stutih mauni santushto yena kena chit — equal to praise and blame, silent, content with whatever comes, homeless, of steady mind, full of devotion. These twelve verses are perhaps the most beloved single passage in the Bhagavad Gita. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 12.13-12.20.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 12 is recited on Day 12 of an 18-day Gita Parayana cycle, but uniquely is also recited daily by countless Vaishnava families and individual sadhakas because it is the shortest and most practical chapter. Specific occasions include: Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), every Ekadashi, Janmashtami (Krishna's birth at midnight), Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi — Mokshada Ekadashi), the morning and evening sandhya, before meals (with Chapter 15), and at family functions. Mahatma Gandhi recited Chapter 12 every evening of his life. The Madhva sampradaya prescribes its daily recitation as essential. The chapter is also chanted for: cultivating bhakti and surrender, peace of mind, freedom from anxiety and depression (the qualities of the devotee verses are a complete mental-health code), and developing equanimity in success and failure. (Sources: Gita Mahatmya; Madhva sampradaya niyamas; Gandhi's daily prayer routine.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chapter 12 connect to the verse-by-verse Gita pages on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 12 — Bhakti Yoga contains 20 verses, each studied verse-by-verse on this site at /gita/chapter-12/verse-1 through /gita/chapter-12/verse-20. The verse-level pages provide the Devanagari, transliteration, word-by-word meaning, English translation, Hindi anvaya and commentary by classical acharyas (Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva, Gyaneshwar) for each shloka — particularly important for the qualities of the devotee verses (12.13-12.20) where each phrase is a complete spiritual instruction. This stotra page presents the entire chapter as a single text for the very popular daily parayana of Bhakti Yoga. Many devotees first learn this chapter by heart, then move on to Chapter 15, then Chapter 2, then Chapter 18 — building up a complete daily Gita practice. (Source: Stotra and Gita parayana traditions.)
